record.field: Teable is a no-code database platform with a spreadsheet-like interface, backed by a real PostgreSQL database.

Teable is a no-code database platform with a spreadsheet-like interface, backed by a real PostgreSQL database.

It positions itself as an open-source Airtable alternative, letting teams build database applications visually while every workspace runs on actual Postgres tables. Because data lives in real Postgres, there are no artificial row limits and you keep full SQL access for BI, ETL and low-code tools. It targets teams that want spreadsheet ease for non-technical users without giving up database performance, scalability and data ownership.

Key features
  • Spreadsheet-like grid plus Kanban, Calendar, Gallery and Form views
  • Real-time collaboration with live cursors and instant updates
  • Full CRUD REST API for every table, plus a React plugin system to extend it
  • Trigger-based automation for workflows
  • AI database agent that creates tables, relationships and workflows from plain-language prompts
  • Self-hosting with full data ownership; external Postgres and S3/MinIO storage support
Best for

Teams and businesses that need an Airtable-style, no-code data tool but want native PostgreSQL performance, large datasets, REST API access and on-premises data control.

field.name: tag_ids
field.eval: [Command.set([ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_self_hosted'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_database'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_no_code'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_collaboration'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_productivity'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_automation'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_ai')])]

record.field: services: teable: image: ghcr.io/teableio/teable:latest restart: always volumes: - ./teable_data:/app/.assets environment: - TZ={{ timezone }} - NEXT_ENV_IMAGES_ALL_REMOTE=true - PUBLIC_ORIGIN={{ public_origin }} - PRISMA_DATABASE_URL={{ prisma_database_url }} - PUBLIC_DATABASE_PROXY={{ public_database_proxy }} - BACKEND_MAIL_HOST=${BACKEND_MAIL_HOST} - BACKEND_MAIL_PORT=${BACKEND_MAIL_PORT} - BACKEND_MAIL_SECURE=${BACKEND_MAIL_SECURE} - BACKEND_MAIL_SENDER=${BACKEND_MAIL_SENDER} - BACKEND_MAIL_SENDER_NAME=${BACKEND_MAIL_SENDER_NAME} - BACKEND_MAIL_AUTH_USER=${BACKEND_MAIL_AUTH_USER} - BACKEND_MAIL_AUTH_PASS=${BACKEND_MAIL_AUTH_PASS} depends_on: teable-db-migrate: condition: service_completed_successfully container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3000' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network teable-db: image: postgres:15.4 restart: always ports: - {{ teable_db_port }}:{{ postgres_port }} volumes: - ./teable_db:/var/lib/postgresql/data environment: - TZ={{ timezone }} - POSTGRES_DB={{ postgres_db }} - POSTGRES_USER={{ postgres_user }} - POSTGRES_PASSWORD={{ postgres_password }} healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - sh -c 'pg_isready -U {{ postgres_user }} -d {{ postgres_db }}' interval: 10s timeout: 3s retries: 3 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_teable_db' networks: - internal teable-db-migrate: image: ghcr.io/teableio/teable-db-migrate:latest environment: - TZ={{ timezone }} - PRISMA_DATABASE_URL=postgresql://{{ postgres_user }}:{{ postgres_password }}@{{ postgres_host }}:{{ postgres_port }}/{{ postgres_db }} depends_on: teable-db: condition: service_healthy container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_teable_db_migrate' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T teable-db sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d teable-db for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T teable-db sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T teable-db sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T teable-db sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
